channel.title: Security on After the Code
channel.description: Recent content in Security on After the Code

item.title: The Limitations of 'Secure' SSRF Patches: Advanced Bypasses and Defense-in-Depth
item.description: A deep dive into why common SSRF defense code is often incomplete, real-world bypasses, and practical, layered mitigation strategies for developers and security engineers.

item.title: Dicer Module Vulnerability Mitigation Guide: CVE-2022-24434
item.description: Mitigation guide for CVE-2022-24434 in the Dicer module affecting Multer and Express, with practical dependency chain fixes.

item.title: How I Managed Unmaintained Open Source with Gmail and Snyk Alerts
item.description: Automating Snyk vulnerability alert management with Google Apps Script and Gmail when official API access falls short.

item.title: There’s No Such Thing as a Free Lunch, But Security Was Free
item.description: The CVE system nearly collapsed in 2025. Who should fund public cybersecurity infrastructure when free-riding is no longer sustainable?

item.title: Common Security Myths Developers Tell Themselves
item.description: Debunking developer security myths around responsibility deflection, tech overconfidence, and risk underestimation with real-world examples.

item.title: XML-RPC Security Vulnerabilities Analysis and Mitigation Strategies
item.description: Comprehensive analysis of XML-RPC security flaws including RCE, XXE, and DDoS, with mitigation strategies and a Python PoC exploit.

item.title: Common Misconceptions of Security Assessors
item.description: Three common misconceptions that weaken security assessments and strategies to build repeatable, effective vulnerability evaluation.
